test_summary: Inspected cmd/tool_github.go and cmd/tool_test.go for GitHub API override policy after two S3 repair passes. Default public API uses ambient GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN; override URLs are normalized HTTPS URLs, must be exactly allowlisted through SLIPWAY_GITHUB_API_ALLOWED_BASE_URLS, require SLIPWAY_GITHUB_API_TOKEN, and do not use ambient tokens. Public https://api.github.com/<path> overrides now fail closed even if allowlisted, including explicit-default-port spellings such as https://api.github.com:443/<path> and mixed-case host input. REST pagination Link URLs are re-authorized before token-bearing follow-up requests and must stay on the configured HTTPS base URL/path. REST and GraphQL both route through githubHTTPClient/newGitHubHTTPClient policy. Passed after second repair: go test ./cmd -run 'TestGitHubAPIOverrideRejectsUnsafeURLs|TestGitHubAPIOverrideRejectsAllowlistedPublicPathConfusion|TestGitHubAPIOverrideRejectsUnsafePaginationLink' -count=1; go test ./cmd -run 'TestGitHubAPIOverride|TestGitHubAPIOverrideRejectsUnsafeURLs|TestGitHubAPIOverrideRequiresOverrideTokenAndDoesNotUseAmbient|TestGitHubAPIOverrideRejectsUnsafePaginationLink' -count=1; git diff --check -- cmd/tool_github.go cmd/tool_test.go artifacts/changes/harden-release-supply-chain/verification/s3-review-repair-notes.md.
notes: Codebase map relevance self-check: ARCHITECTURE/STRUCTURE/TESTING/CONCERNS were relevant only to confirm cmd/tool_github.go as the shared REST/GraphQL token-policy choke point and cmd/tool_test.go plus the focused test regex as the intended verification surface; no broader map-driven scope was needed. S3 repair batch s3-review-repair:harden-release-supply-chain closed public GitHub path-confusion, public GitHub default-port path-confusion, and pagination Link token-leak blockers with dedicated regression tests. Second repair subagent: 019f04e3-aa63-7ed2-a61f-467180f45be5.
